use std::collections::HashMap;

struct TemplateEngine {
    templates: HashMap<String, CompiledTemplate>,
}

struct CompiledTemplate {
    name: String,
    bytecode: Vec<u8>,
}

fn build_engine() -> TemplateEngine {
    let mut templates = HashMap::new();
    // BAD: compile all templates eagerly, even unused ones
    let all_templates = vec![
        ("home", "Hello {{name}}"),
        ("admin", "Admin: {{name}}"),
        ("report", "Report: {{data}}"),
        ("email", "Dear {{name}}, {{body}}"),
        ("error", "Error: {{code}}: {{message}}"),
        ("invoice", "Invoice #{{id}}: {{amount}}"),
    ];

    for (name, source) in all_templates {
        templates.insert(
            name.to_string(),
            CompiledTemplate {
                name: name.to_string(),
                bytecode: compile_template(source),
            },
        );
    }

    TemplateEngine { templates }
}

fn compile_template(source: &str) -> Vec<u8> {
    source.bytes().collect()
}
